title: FLTA hypothetical: cross-device FL on BloodMNIST under FL + TEE + DP-C
description: Hypothetical cross-device federated learning on the MedMNIST v2 BloodMNIST benchmark — 200 data subjects across the EU contributing pod-resident blood-cell-image slices to a federated classifier. Aggregation runs inside a TEE on an EU cloud region; the released model satisfies a stated central-DP bound. This card is the worked example accompanying the FLTA 2026 short paper.

pet_components.role: Local data retention — each subject's pod hosts their imaging slice; only DP-noised model updates leave the pod.

tooling.name: Flower
tooling.notes: Reference client/server framework; the evaluation harness uses a minimal numpy FedAvg implementation for reproducibility (see flta_eval/fl.py).

pet_components.parameter_notes: Cross-device FL pattern per Kairouz et al. (Foundations and Trends in ML, 2021) §3. Non-IID partition: Dirichlet(α=0.5) per Hsu et al. 2019.

pet_components.role: Hardware isolation of aggregation server — enclave protects intermediate model state from the cloud operator.

parameters.side_channel_mitigations: memory encryption enabled; no hyperthreading on enclave vCPUs

pet_components.parameter_notes: Per-round attestation; measurement pinned in change-control log.

pet_components.role: Formal output disclosure bound — Gaussian mechanism applied to per-client gradients before aggregation; RDP composition across rounds.
pet_components.parameter_notes: ε=2.0 is chosen at or below the gradient-inversion threshold (ε*=4.0) pinned to Hatamizadeh et al. (CVPR 2023) and Boenisch et al. (USENIX Security 2023). Composition across 30 rounds tracked via the RDP accountant; ε is the upper bound reported by the accountant for δ=1e-5.

trust_model.summary: Data subjects are EU residents who have authorised participation via consent receipt stored on their Solid pod. The aggregation server is operated by an EU cloud provider and is treated as honest-but-curious; the TEE isolates aggregation from the host. External analysts are untrusted. The released model is public output bounded by the declared DP guarantee.

threat_actors.capability: observes per-client update gradients before the TEE-internal sum
threat_actors.mitigated_by: TEE isolates intermediate state from the host OS; DP-C bounds released-model leakage
threat_actors.residual_risk: Per-client visibility inside the TEE remains; gradient-inversion residual at ε=2.0 is bounded by the SQ-1 calibration measurement (see risk_calibration.attack_target).

threat_actors.capability: queries the released model; has bounded auxiliary data
threat_actors.mitigated_by: DP-C guarantee plus the operational attack-rate bound in risk_calibration
threat_actors.residual_risk: Worst-record MIA at FPR=10⁻³ bounded at the measured target_advantage.

out_of_scope_threats: Supply-chain compromise of the FL framework or DP library

required_artefacts.artefact: RDP accountant log + DP parameter manifest
FL training protocol + per-round audit log

assurance_targets.maturity_justification: Workflow is repeatable and documented. Stage 3 requires formal release log + reviewer sign-off pathway.

governance_controls.access_control: Per-pod WAC/ACP rules; each subject grants the FL client read access to their data resource only.
governance_controls.change_control: Parameter changes (ε, clipping, aggregation strategy) require change-control log entry + governance review.

applicable_regulations.gap_notes: Classification depends on downstream deployment; the BloodMNIST benchmark itself sits in research scope.

standards_alignment.notes: RDP accountant satisfies ε-tracking; per-release sensitivity analysis not yet exercised.

jurisdictional_context.notes: Coherent single-regime deployment: all participants are EU residents; controller and operator are EU. No cross-border mechanism required at this configuration. A future US-region operator step would require SCCs or adequacy and would trigger COMP-JURIS-001 until declared.

risk_calibration.operational_interpretation: Worst-record MIA against the FL-released model bounded at TPR=0.030 at FPR=10⁻³ for the budget-bounded intruder profile, calibrated against the SQ-1 result record in evidence_ref. The (ε=2, δ=10⁻⁵) configured DP guarantee corresponds to µ-DP ≈ 0.85 via the Gaussian-DP conversion (Dong, Roth & Su 2022).
risk_calibration.notes: ε=2.0 is the operational choice: at or below the gradient-inversion threshold ε*=4.0 pinned to Hatamizadeh et al. (CVPR 2023) and Boenisch et al. (USENIX Security 2023). The measured target_advantage from the SQ-1 notebook may exceed the declared 0.030 at tutorial scale — that is expected (the small-sweep worst-record max is high-variance) and would fire RISKCAL-002; paper-spec configuration (n_targets=60, n_shadow_runs=128) converges to the stable bound.

stepwise_chain.name: Baseline — completely private
stepwise_chain.purpose: Starting state. Each data subject's imaging slice sits in their Solid pod under WAC/ACP control; no FL client has been granted read access.
stepwise_chain.trust_assumption_added: trivial — each subject trusts their own pod's access-control policy
stepwise_chain.assurance_anchor: WAC/ACP rules on each pod; WebID-OIDC authentication
stepwise_chain.residual_risk: Trivially private; trivially useless for federated training.

stepwise_chain.name: Authorise FL clients to read pod-resident data
stepwise_chain.purpose: Each subject grants the FL client read access to their data resource via a WAC/ACP rule, evidenced by a signed Consent Receipt.
stepwise_chain.trust_assumption_added: honest-but-curious coordinator; participating clients honour pod access-control
stepwise_chain.assurance_anchor: FL training protocol; per-round participation log written back to each pod (participation.jsonld)
stepwise_chain.residual_risk: Per-update gradient leakage [Geiping 2020; Zhu 2019]; coordinator observes per-client updates.

stepwise_chain.name: Bound per-update leakage via central DP
stepwise_chain.purpose: Address per-update leakage; bound what the released model reveals about any individual subject.
stepwise_chain.trust_assumption_added: trusted curator (the aggregator); named composition theorem (RDP)
stepwise_chain.assurance_anchor: RDP accountant log; DP parameter manifest; risk_calibration block with measured target_advantage
stepwise_chain.residual_risk: Coordinator still observes DP-noised individual updates. accepted residual: gradient inversion residual at ε=2.0 — bounded by the gradient-inversion calibration in SQ-1 (a per-input PSNR threshold above which reconstruction is considered successful).

stepwise_chain.name: Protect aggregation environment
stepwise_chain.purpose: Aggregator runs on cloud infrastructure; protect data-in-use from the cloud operator and the host OS.
stepwise_chain.assurance_anchor: per-round attestation report; enclave measurement; side-channel mitigation declaration
stepwise_chain.residual_risk: Hardware vendor compromise; Spectre-class side channels not covered by declared mitigations. accepted residual: coordinator visibility inside the enclave — closing this would require S-03 (FL + MPC + DP-C); the cost/benefit is workshop-pending.

change_log.change: FLTA 2026 example chain. Cross-device FL on BloodMNIST under a single EU regulatory frame. risk_calibration.attack_target populated with the operational MIA bound; gradient-inversion residual explicitly accepted with reference to the ε threshold from Hatamizadeh 2023 / Boenisch 2023.
change_log.author: Privacy Explorer
